q: In which organisms does photosynthesis occur?
options: All living organisms, since all require glucose for respiration
Only flowering plants and mosses that possess true leaves
Plants, algae, and cyanobacteria — all of which contain chlorophyll or related pigments
Any organism living in sunlit environments, including aerobic bacteria and fungi
explanation: Photosynthesis (oxygenic) occurs in plants, algae, and cyanobacteria; these organisms share chlorophyll-based pigments and the ability to split water and fix CO2.
